speaker: Mike
content: Bongo, get down.

speaker: Eddie
content: Wait, let me show you something.
mark: let me show you something 让我给你看些东西

speaker: Mike
content: He loves that gravy.
mark: gravy n. 肉汁；不法利润；轻易得来的钱； 作为肉汁肉卤时是不可数名词

speaker: Eddie
content: Come, while you were in there tossing a salad, I taught him something.
mark: toss v. 扔，抛，掷；（使）摇摆，挥动，颠簸；搅拌（食物），拌匀
toss a salad 拌一份沙拉 salad 是可数名词
teach sb sth 教某人某事 -> taught -> taught

speaker: Alice
content: Susan was furious with Eddie for using a dog to ingratiate herself with its owner.
mark: furious adj. 狂怒的，暴怒的；狂暴的，猛烈的；喧闹的，热烈兴奋的
be furious with sb for doing sth 因某人做了某事而对其大发雷霆
ingratiate vt. 使迎合；使讨好；使逢迎
use sth to ingratiate oneself with sb 利用某物来讨好某人

speaker: Alice
content: She was also furious with Mike for not seeing through this blatant maneuver.
mark: see through 指能够透过看到背后的事物。
blatant adj. 喧嚣的；公然的；炫耀的；俗丽的
maneuver(美式) = manoeuvre(英式) n. 细致巧妙的移动，机动动作；策略，手段；军事演习
blatant maneuver用于描述那些不道德、不正当且毫不掩饰的行为策略

speaker: Alice
content: But mostly she was furious with herself for not having thought of it first.

speaker: Susan
content: so, you know what? I'm gonna go get the desserts.
mark: go get sth 去拿某物，去取某物；两个动词连用更加简洁、直接，口语化

speaker: Eddie
content: You are such a smart boy!

speaker: Mike
content: I can barely get him to sit. I've never seen him walk on two legs.
mark: barely adv. 勉强才能；几乎不，几乎没有；刚刚；仅仅，只有；
I can barely get him to sit 我几乎没办法让他坐下。get sb. to do sth 强调通过某种努力、办法或手段去促使某人做某事
I can barely let him sit 我几乎不太愿意让他坐下。let sb. do sth. 更侧重于表达一种许可、准许的态度
have never done和haven't done虽然都表示没有做某事，但have never done 侧重于强调从来没有，而haven't done更注重说明当下事情的未完成状态。
see sb do sth 看见某人做某事

speaker: Eddie
content: He knows who to come to, doesn’t he?
mark: come to sb 向某人求助；去找某人

speaker: Susan
content: Here's your dessert.

speaker: Eddie
content: Thank you, Mike, this looks yummy.

speaker: Mike
content: Oh, look at that, it’s like Bongo's finally warming up to Susan.
mark: warm up to 逐渐接受或喜欢某人或某事物

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, we just got off on the wrong foot, we're best buddies now. Oh, what a good boy! Bongo, are you OK?
mark: get off on the wrong foot 一开始就做错；一开始就不顺利；出师不利
buddy n. 伙伴，好朋友
what a good boy! what引导的感叹句: What + (a/an) + 形容词 + 名词 (+ 主语 + 谓语)! == what a good boy (he is)!
可以转换为how引导的感叹句: How + (形容词 / 副词) + 主语 + 谓语！ == how good the boy is.

speaker: Eddie
content: What's wrong with him?

speaker: Mike
content: I don't know, I never heard him make that sound. Are you OK, fella?
mark: fella n. 伙伴，伙计；小伙子

speaker: Julie
content: Mom? Where is your earring?

speaker: Mike
content: Call the vet, the number's on the fridge, tell him I'll be right there.
mark: vet n. 兽医
fridge n. 冰箱
I'll be right there. 我马上就到

speaker: Eddie
content: OK, don't worry about a thing. I'll stay here and get everything cleaned up.
mark: Don't worry about a thing 别担心任何事，什么都不用担心。== Don't worry about anything
get sth done 完成某事；使某事被做

speaker: Mike
content: Thanks, Eddie.

speaker: Susan
content: Is there anything I can do?

speaker: Mike
content: No.
